evidence_quote: CAUSAL for albinism. Per-allele (1st allele) effect -7.7 MI (p=2.2E-5, Table2); -7.744 MI (p=9.0E-7, App1-T5). Freq 0.03. HW albinism freq ~1/1000 matches observed 3/~3000
classification_notes: OCA2 canonical; NW273KV albinism allele.
author_explanation_quote: we identify the new albinism allele...NW sequence among vertebrates and its inclusion in a potential N-linked glycosylation site...eliminated by the mutation supports the variant's pathogenicity
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion

evidence_quote: -5.8 MI/allele (p=1.5E-12, Table2); -5.757 MI (p=2.45E-16, App1-T5, b37 pos in App2). Only variant reaching genome-wide significance
classification_notes: SLC24A5 canonical; A111T.
author_explanation_quote: The effect size for SLC24A5-A111T measured here is consistent with previously reported results...causal skin-lightening variant (Lamason et al., 2005)
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion

classification_notes: SLC45A2 canonical; L374F hypomorph.
author_explanation_quote: A significant effect of SLC45A2-L374F on skin pigmentation reported for the African-American sample by Norton et al., 2007...similar to the 5 unit effect reported
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion

classification_notes: Sex is a covariate, not a genetic locus -- excluded from effector classification.
author_explanation_quote: Estimated effect sizes for significant covariates were little affected by the inclusion of age as a covariate
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion
notes: Non-genetic covariate; 'mechanism' here = known demographic covariate, not a genetic mechanism claim.

locus_id: Native American genetic ancestry fraction (genome-wide admixture)
evidence_quote: slope -24.3 MI per unit NAM (MI=-24.3*NAM+61.9; r2=0.2722). PC1 (NAM vs AFR) beta -62.14, p=5.73E-32 (App1-T5)
classification_notes: NAM (Native American) ancestry fraction is an admixture covariate, not a genetic locus -- excluded.
author_explanation_quote: Native American genetic ancestry alone caused a measurable effect on pigmentation
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion

locus_id: chr4 ~1.7 Mb shared homozygous albino segment (co-segregating passengers)
nearest_gene_label: EMCN / PPP3CA / SLC39A8 / MANBA / TACR3 (chr4 segment members)
evidence_quote: albinism-candidate co-segregating with OCA2 albino haplotype (~1.7 Mb shared homozygous segment); not causal
classification_notes: Chr4 ~1.7Mb shared homozygous segment (IBD co-segregation with albino cases); none of the 5 candidate genes are canonical; authors do not demonstrate an effect on any single gene -- passengers by co-segregation only. Candidate for LD-nomination against network genes.
author_explanation_quote: All three albino individuals share a homozygous segment of ~1.7 Mb that encompasses several genes in addition to OCA2
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion
notes: Authors explicitly attribute passenger status to co-segregation with the causal OCA2 allele by IBD, not an independent pigmentation mechanism for these genes; classified nearest_gene_only because no gene-specific mechanism is given for EMCN/PPP3CA/SLC39A8/MANBA/TACR3 themselves.

evidence_quote: albinism-candidate co-segregating with OCA2 albino haplotype (~1.7 Mb shared homozygous segment); not causal
classification_notes: LRMDA not on canonical list but mygene.info confirms: 'leucine rich melanocyte differentiation associated...thought to play a role in melanocyte differentiation. Mutations...associated with autosomal recessive oculocutaneous albinism 7 (OCA7)' -- strong independent functional evidence despite absence from the task's canonical panel. Ang2023 itself gives no locus-specific mechanism (passenger by co-segregation), but LRMDA's OCA7 link is external corroboration worth flagging.
author_explanation_quote: All three albino individuals share a homozygous segment of ~1.7 Mb that encompasses several genes in addition to OCA2
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion
notes: Same co-segregating-passenger logic as chr4 segment; no LRMDA-specific mechanism given.

locus_id: chr15 OCA2 albino-haplotype tag SNPs (non-coding passengers)
nearest_gene_label: OCA2 (non-coding/synonymous tag SNPs on albino haplotype)
evidence_quote: albinism-candidate co-segregating with OCA2 albino haplotype (~1.7 Mb shared homozygous segment); not causal
classification_notes: OCA2 canonical; albino-haplotype tag SNPs.
author_explanation_quote: The albino haplotype defined by homozygosity in individuals 2 and 3 extends ~11 Mb
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion
notes: Tag SNPs on the albino haplotype; carried by LD/co-inheritance with causal OCA2-NW273KV, not independently mechanistic.

evidence_quote: NOT causal for skin color; scores INCORRECTLY as pathogenic by SIFT/Polyphen2.0/PANTHER; no detectable effect when controlling for NW273KV. Freq 0.068 (b37 pos 28260053). CADD 22.7
classification_notes: OCA2 canonical; R305W, mechanism_proposed for ABSENCE of effect after conditioning on NW273KV.
author_explanation_quote: when controlling for OCA2-NW273KV status, OCA2-R305W had no detectable effect on skin color... R305W scores incorrectly as pathogenic using SIFT, Polyphen 2.0, and PANTHER
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion
notes: Mechanism_proposed for absence-of-effect: authors give an explicit functional argument (in silico pathogenicity tools mis-score it; no detectable phenotypic effect after conditioning) rather than leaving it merely unexplained.

locus_id: SYT6 locus, chr1 ~114.5-114.6 Mb (~20 SNPs collapsed to representative)
rsid: rs116218201 (representative); cluster also rs113236485, rs115102845, rs185469828
evidence_quote: Meets 5e-8 in LMM but MAF<2% (inconsistent with AFR/NAM-differentiating causal variant); authors: does NOT warrant further investigation. SYT6 locus = ~20 SNPs w/ identical stats collapsed to representatives
classification_notes: SYT6 not canonical; ~20-SNP cluster collapsed to one representative, MAF<2% deemed inconsistent with being the causal variant by the authors themselves -- no demonstrated gene-level effect.
author_explanation_quote: our interpretation is that none of these variants warrant further investigation. Low observed minor allele frequencies (<2%) are inconsistent with those expected for variants responsible for pigmentation differences
author_explanation_section: Results and discussion

classification_notes: HERC2 canonical (task's own flagged example rs12913832). CRITICAL: Ang2023's own text gives NO locus-specific mechanistic discussion for this locus in this population (P_adj=0.07497, not significant) -- the well-known HERC2-OCA2-enhancer mechanism is established in OTHER papers (e.g. Abbatangelo2026 idx0, Morgan2018 idx78), not asserted by Ang2023 itself. Still classified canonical_effector_variant_gap because HERC2 IS the attributed gene and IS on the canonical list -- the task's rule is 'gene is canonical -> out of scope', independent of whether THIS paper explains the mechanism. This is the exact case the re-extraction was commissioned to fix (previously mis-tagged 'author-unexplained').
author_explanation_quote: 15 28365618 rs12913832 A G HERC2 near OCA2 intronic 15.8 - - ... (no Polyphen/SIFT); 15 28380518 rs4778249 T A HERC2 ...
author_explanation_section: Results (Appendix 2 - Table of previously reported variants)
notes: Ang2023 itself gives no locus-specific mechanistic discussion for rs12913832 in this population (it appears only in the previously-reported-variants table, P_adj=0.075, not significant); the well-known HERC2-enhancer-regulates-OCA2 mechanism is established in OTHER papers (e.g. Abbatangelo2026), not asserted by Ang2023's own text.

classification_notes: MFSD12 NOT on the task's canonical list, but mygene.info confirms explicit pigmentation function: 'pigment metabolic process involved in pigmentation; regulation of melanin biosynthetic process...located in lysosome and melanosome.' Coding variant Y182H, probably_damaging(0.999); not significant in Ang2023 alone (P_adj=0.0723) but Crawford2017 (idx43) independently reports a significant, CRISPR-validated MFSD12 signal -- strong cross-paper corroboration for a non-canonical but demonstrated effector.
author_explanation_quote: 19 3548231 rs2240751 A G MFSD12 Y182H ... probably_damaging(0.999) deleterious(0)
author_explanation_section: Introduction; Results (Appendix 2, Table 1)
notes: Introduction also cites Adhikari et al. 2019 finding a significant Y182H-skin color association, contextualizing it as a validated but population-restricted effect (freq 0.27/0.17 in NAM/EAS).

classification_notes: ATRN not canonical; mygene.info: 'mahogany protein homolog (mouse)' -- known pigmentation-adjacent gene in mouse coat colour (agouti/mahogany pathway) though human pigmentation function not explicitly stated in the summary; not significant in this locus. Zhang2018 melanocyte eQTL cross-check (Supp Table S6): gene is present in the melanin/pigment GO or 379-curated-gene test set but did NOT reach significance as a melanocyte eGene (q-value not reported/non-significant).
author_explanation_quote: intronic/downstream annotation only, no coding-effect prediction tabulated
author_explanation_section: Results (Appendix 2 - Table of previously reported variants)

classification_notes: ASIP IS canonical; AHCY is not. Label is 3'UTR/intron, ambiguous which gene is targeted; not significant. Treated as canonical-neighbour candidate given ASIP's presence and established role, pending demonstrated-effect confirmation. Zhang2018 melanocyte eQTL cross-check (Supp Table S6): ASIP tested in melanin-GO gene set but did not reach eGene significance in melanocytes (blank q-value) -- note this cross-check is on ASIP's own melanocyte cis-eQTL signal, distinct from the RALY-intronic variant's eQTL-for-ASIP claim made directly by Morgan2018 (idx80), which used a different eQTL dataset/tissue.
author_explanation_quote: 3'UTR/intron annotation only, no coding-effect prediction tabulated
